prompt: Will Swift's do/catch handle all errors in my iOS app? Are there exceptions it won't catch?

assertions.text: States that Objective-C NSExceptions are NOT caught by Swift do/catch
assertions.rationale: Fundamental gap in Swift error handling — skill covers this explicitly

assertions.text: Lists specific NSException triggers (NSArray out-of-bounds, invalid KVO, unrecognized selector)
assertions.rationale: Skill provides actionable examples — baseline may be vague

assertions.text: Mentions that Swift traps (force unwrap nil) also bypass do/catch (caught by signal handlers only)
assertions.rationale: Skill's error type coverage table — shows the full picture
